title: Getting Started with AccumulatR
text: analyses AccumulatR follow four steps: Define model accumulators, pools, observed responses. Finalize model finalize_model(). Work behavioral data, either simulated simulate() collected experiment. Build likelihood context evaluate candidate parameter values.

title: Getting Started with AccumulatR
text: trial single behavioral observation. simplest case, row data corresponds one trial.

title: Getting Started with AccumulatR
text: R observed response label trial. two-choice task, typical values might "left" "right", "go" "stop".

title: Getting Started with AccumulatR
text: rt observed response time R.

title: Getting Started with AccumulatR
text: use race_spec(n_outcomes = 2), model keeps first two ordered responses trial. case: R rt first observed response response time. R2 rt2 second observed response response time. logic extends R3, rt3, beyond ordered outcomes retained.

title: Getting Started with AccumulatR
text: component latent submodel inside mixture model. useful think behavior comes qualitatively different trial types, : fast-guess process trials controlled decision process others model one component, usually need think column .

title: Getting Started with AccumulatR
text: onset time accumulator becomes active. accumulators start time 0, others begin later. AccumulatR, onset can : fixed delay, onset = 0.15 chained onset, onset = ("cue"), meaning process starts another accumulator pool finished

title: Getting Started with AccumulatR
text: example defines basic two-choice race model.
code: spec <- race_spec() |>   add_accumulator("left", "lognormal") |>   add_accumulator("right", "lognormal") |>   add_outcome("left", "left") |>   add_outcome("right", "right")  model <- finalize_model(spec)

title: Getting Started with AccumulatR
text: can generate small behavioral dataset model. row one trial, R observed response, rt response time.
code: pars <- c(   left.m = log(0.28), left.s = 0.16, left.q = 0, left.t0 = 0,   right.m = log(0.35), right.s = 0.18, right.q = 0, right.t0 = 0 )  param_df <- build_param_matrix(spec, pars, n_trials = 6) sim <- simulate(model, param_df, seed = 123)  sim[c("trial", "R", "rt")] ##   trial     R        rt ## 1     1  left 0.3182631 ## 2     2  left 0.3414185 ## 3     3  left 0.2883234 ## 4     4  left 0.3669476 ## 5     5  left 0.3057125 ## 6     6 right 0.2456584

title: Getting Started with AccumulatR
text: fit model, usual pattern : build_likelihood_context() prepares behavioral data repeated likelihood evaluation. log_likelihood() tells well parameter set explains observed data.

title: Getting Started with AccumulatR
text: want basic fitting workflow, read simple race-model vignette. experiment records ordered responses, read multi-outcome vignette. model staged processing, read chained-onset vignette.

what: Simulation and Likelihood Tools for Evidence-Accumulation Models
title: Simulation and Likelihood Tools for Evidence-Accumulation Models
text: AccumulatR R/C++ toolkit race-model simulation likelihood evaluation. built kinds models cognitive psychologists use responses generated competing evidence-accumulation processes. can define model, simulate behavioral data, evaluate well different parameter values explain observed response times choices.

title: Simulation and Likelihood Tools for Evidence-Accumulation Models
text: Install released version CRAN: install current development version GitHub:

title: Simulation and Likelihood Tools for Evidence-Accumulation Models
text: AccumulatR organized around simple workflow: Define model accumulators, pools, observed responses. Turn specification object ready simulation fitting. Simulate behavioral data known parameter values. Evaluate log-likelihood observed data candidate parameters.

title: Simulation and Likelihood Tools for Evidence-Accumulation Models
text: example defines two-choice race model, simulates response-time data, evaluates likelihood data generating parameters. simulate() returns behavioral data one row per trial. simplest case means response column (R) response-time column (rt). log_likelihood() evaluates probable data set model parameters. longer worked examples, see package articles pkgdown site.
code: library(AccumulatR)  spec <- race_spec() |>   add_accumulator("left", "lognormal") |>   add_accumulator("right", "lognormal") |>   add_outcome("left", "left") |>   add_outcome("right", "right")  model <- finalize_model(spec)  pars <- c(   left.m = log(0.28), left.s = 0.16, left.q = 0, left.t0 = 0,   right.m = log(0.35), right.s = 0.18, right.q = 0, right.t0 = 0 )  param_df <- build_param_matrix(spec, pars, n_trials = 8) sim <- simulate(model, param_df, seed = 123) head(sim[c("trial", "R", "rt")])  ctx <- build_likelihood_context(model, sim[c("trial", "R", "rt")]) log_likelihood(ctx, param_df)

title: AccumulatR: Simulate and fit evidence-accumulation models — AccumulatR-package
text: `AccumulatR` researchers work response-time models behavior generated competing interacting accumulators. package lets describe model psychologically meaningful terms, simulate behavioral data, evaluate likelihoods fitting. main workflow : 1. Start `race_spec()`. 2. Add accumulators, pools, outcomes, timing relations. 3. Call `finalize_model()` prepare model simulation fitting. 4. Use `simulate()`, `build_likelihood_context()`, `log_likelihood()`. R interface intended stay readable model-focused, repeated numerical work happens C++.

title: AccumulatR: Simulate and fit evidence-accumulation models — AccumulatR-package
text: Maintainer: Niek Stevenson niek.stevenson@gmail.com

title: Prepare behavioral data for repeated likelihood evaluation — build_likelihood_context
text: structure Finalized model structure. data_df Behavioral data. simplest case contains `trial`, `R`, `rt`; multi-outcome models can also contain `R2`, `rt2`, . prep Optional preprocessed model bundle. native_bundle Optional serialized native bundle.

title: Prepare behavioral data for repeated likelihood evaluation — build_likelihood_context
text: `likelihood_context` object.

title: Prepare behavioral data for repeated likelihood evaluation — build_likelihood_context
code: spec <- race_spec() spec <- add_accumulator(spec, "A", "lognormal") spec <- add_outcome(spec, "A_win", "A") structure <- finalize_model(spec) params_df <- build_param_matrix(   spec,   c(A.m = 0, A.s = 0.1, A.q = 0, A.t0 = 0),   n_trials = 2 ) data_df <- simulate(structure, params_df, seed = 1) build_likelihood_context(structure, data_df) #> $structure #> $model_spec #> $accumulators #> code: spec <- race_spec() spec <- add_accumulator(spec, "A", "lognormal") spec <- add_outcome(spec, "A_win", "A") vals <- c(A.m = 0, A.s = 0.1, A.q = 0, A.t0 = 0) build_param_matrix(spec, vals, n_trials = 2) #>      q w t0 p1  p2 p3 #> [1,] 0 1  0  0 0.1  0 #> [2,] 0 1  0  0 0.1  0

$outcomes[[1]]$label #> [1] "A_win" #>  #> $outcomes[[1]]$expr #> $outcomes[[1]]$expr$kind #> [1] "event" #>  #> $outcomes[[1]]$expr$source #> [1] "A" #>  #> $outcomes[[1]]$expr$k #> NULL #>  #>  #> $prep$accumulators$A$shared_trigger_q #> NULL #>  #>  #>  #> $prep$pools #> named list() #>  #> $prep$outcomes #> $prep$outcomes$A_win #> $prep$outcomes$A_win$label #> [1] "A_win" #>  #> title: Evaluate the log-likelihood of behavioral data — log_likelihood
text: likelihood_context Context created `build_likelihood_context()`. parameters parameter data frame, list parameter data frames. ok Logical vector marking trials contribute likelihood. Trials marked `FALSE` assigned `min_ll`. expand Optional index vector used expand compressed trial-level results back original trial count. min_ll Minimum log-likelihood value used excluded impossible trials. ... Unused; S3 compatibility.

title: Evaluate the log-likelihood of behavioral data — log_likelihood

title: Evaluate the log-likelihood of behavioral data — log_likelihood
code: spec <- race_spec() spec <- add_accumulator(spec, "A", "lognormal") spec <- add_outcome(spec, "A_win", "A") structure <- finalize_model(spec) params_df <- build_param_matrix(   spec,   c(A.m = 0, A.s = 0.1, A.q = 0, A.t0 = 0),   n_trials = 2 ) data_df <- simulate(structure, params_df, seed = 1) ctx <- build_likelihood_context(structure, data_df) log_likelihood(ctx, list(params_df)) #> [1] 2.481128

what: Plot accumulators and their timing relations — plot_accumulators
title: Plot accumulators and their timing relations — plot_accumulators
text: Draw schematic view model time x-axis evidence y-axis. accumulator shown simple trajectory, timing blocking relations overlaid can inspect qualitative structure model fitting.

title: Plot accumulators and their timing relations — plot_accumulators
code: plot_accumulators(   model,   xlim = NULL,   ylim = c(0, 1),   line_length = 1,   angle_dodge = 10,   accumulator_order = NULL,   curve_strength = 0.18,   line_col = "gray20",   line_lwd = 3,   axis_lwd = 3,   link_lwd = 2.2,   blocker_lwd = 2.6,   show_labels = TRUE,   main = "Accumulator Schematic",   xlab = "Time",   ylab = "Evidence",   ... )

title: Plot accumulators and their timing relations — plot_accumulators
text: model race model (`race_spec`, finalized model, `model_tables`). xlim Optional x-axis limits. `NULL`, chosen model. ylim Y-axis limits. line_length Length accumulator trajectory plot units. angle_dodge Target angular spacing degrees neighbouring accumulators. Angles centered 45 degrees constrained interval [10, 80]. many accumulators sharing onset, spacing reduced uniformly within onset group. accumulator_order Optional character vector accumulator labels, ordered top--bottom. affects accumulators sharing onset. curve_strength Curvature applied relationship arcs. line_col Color accumulator trajectories. line_lwd Line width accumulator trajectories. axis_lwd Line width x/y axis arrows. link_lwd Reserved reciprocal relationship arrows (currently disabled). blocker_lwd Line width blocker arrows. show_labels `TRUE`, add accumulator labels near line endpoints. main Main title. xlab X-axis label. ylab Y-axis label. ... Additional arguments passed `graphics::plot`.

title: Plot accumulators and their timing relations — plot_accumulators
text: Invisibly returns list plotting coordinates inferred   structural links.

title: Plot accumulators and their timing relations — plot_accumulators
code: spec <- race_spec() |>   add_accumulator("go", "lognormal") |>   add_accumulator("stop", "lognormal", onset = 0.15) |>   add_pool("P", c("go", "stop")) |>   add_outcome("R", "P") |>   finalize_model() plot_accumulators(spec)

code: spec <- race_spec() |>   add_accumulator("go", "lognormal") |>   add_accumulator("stop", "lognormal") |>   add_outcome("go", "go") |>   add_outcome("stop", "stop") |>   set_parameters(list(     drift = c("go.m", "stop.m"),     spread = c("go.s", "stop.s"),     onset = "go.t0"   ))  sampled_pars(spec) #> [1] "drift"   "spread"  "go.q"    "onset"   "stop.q"  "stop.t0"

what: Simulate behavioral data from a model — simulate
title: Simulate behavioral data from a model — simulate
text: Simulate behavioral data model

title: Simulate behavioral data from a model — simulate
code: simulate(   structure,   params_df,   trial_df = NULL,   seed = NULL,   keep_detail = FALSE,   keep_component = NULL,   layout = c("auto", "rectangular", "long"),   ... )  # S3 method for class 'model_structure' simulate(   structure,   params_df,   trial_df = NULL,   seed = NULL,   keep_detail = FALSE,   keep_component = NULL,   layout = c("auto", "rectangular", "long"),   ... )  # Default S3 method simulate(structure, ...)

title: Simulate behavioral data from a model — simulate
text: structure Finalized model structure. params_df Trial-level parameter values. trial_df Optional data frame used condition simulation. includes `accumulator` column, `onset` `component` values matched trial accumulator. Otherwise, values apply trial level. seed Optional random-number seed. keep_detail `TRUE`, keep additional simulation detail. keep_component Whether keep chosen mixture component output model multiple components. `NULL`, fixed mixtures keep component label sampled mixtures drop . layout Parameter layout. `"rectangular"` expects rows ordered trial accumulator. `"long"` expects rows aligned `trial_df` inferable `component`. `"auto"` chooses automatically. ... Unused; S3 compatibility.

title: Simulate behavioral data from a model — simulate
text: data frame simulated behavioral data. standard models   includes `trial`, `R`, `rt`. `n_outcomes > 1`, additional ordered   response columns `R2`/`rt2` included.

title: Simulate behavioral data from a model — simulate
code: spec <- race_spec() spec <- add_accumulator(spec, "A", "lognormal") spec <- add_outcome(spec, "A_win", "A") structure <- finalize_model(spec) params <- c(A.m = 0, A.s = 0.1, A.q = 0, A.t0 = 0) df <- build_param_matrix(spec, params, n_trials = 3) simulate(structure, df, seed = 123) #>   trial     R       rt #> 1     1 A_win 1.083347 #> 2     2 A_win 1.168675 #> 3     3 A_win 1.131959

title: AccumulatR 0.1.0
text: First public release. Added tools defining evidence-accumulation models accumulators, pools, response rules, mixture components, chained onsets. Added simulation helpers generating behavioral data model specifications. Added likelihood tools evaluating observed response-time data candidate parameter values. Added pkgdown articles reference documentation main modeling workflow.
